response:
```python
# test_rate_limiter.py

import time

import pytest

from your_module import rate_limiter  # Replace with the actual module name


class FakeClock:
    def __init__(self, start=1000.0):
        self.now = start

    def time(self):
        return self.now

    def advance(self, seconds):
        self.now += seconds


@pytest.fixture
def fake_clock(monkeypatch):
    clock = FakeClock()
    monkeypatch.setattr(time, "time", clock.time)
    return clock


def test_allows_calls_up_to_max_within_period(fake_clock):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=3, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is True

    assert allow() is False


def test_allows_again_after_period_expires(fake_clock):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=2, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is False

    fake_clock.advance(10)

    assert allow() is True


def test_uses_sliding_window_not_fixed_window(fake_clock):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=2, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is True  # t = 1000

    fake_clock.advance(5)
    assert allow() is True  # t = 1005

    fake_clock.advance(4)
    assert allow() is False  # t = 1009, both calls still in window

    fake_clock.advance(1)
    assert allow() is True  # t = 1010, first call expired

    fake_clock.advance(4)
    assert allow() is False  # t = 1014, calls at 1005 and 1010 still count

    fake_clock.advance(1)
    assert allow() is True  # t = 1015, call at 1005 expired


def test_call_exactly_at_period_boundary_is_allowed(fake_clock):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is False

    fake_clock.advance(10)

    assert allow() is True


def test_call_just_before_period_boundary_is_rejected(fake_clock):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is True

    fake_clock.advance(9.999)

    assert allow() is False


def test_limiters_have_independent_state(fake_clock):
    allow_a = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=10)
    allow_b = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow_a() is True
    assert allow_a() is False

    assert allow_b() is True
    assert allow_b() is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize("max_calls", [0, -1])
def test_non_positive_max_calls_never_allows(fake_clock, max_calls):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=max_calls, period_seconds=10)

    assert allow() is False

    fake_clock.advance(100)

    assert allow() is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize("period_seconds", [0, -1])
def test_non_positive_period_does_not_retain_previous_calls(fake_clock, period_seconds):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=period_seconds)

    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is True
    assert allow() is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize("max_calls", ["1", None, 1.5])
def test_invalid_max_calls_type_raises_type_error(fake_clock, max_calls):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=max_calls, period_seconds=10)

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        allow()


@pytest.mark.parametrize("period_seconds", ["10", None])
def test_invalid_period_seconds_type_raises_type_error_on_second_call(
    fake_clock,
    period_seconds,
):
    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=period_seconds)

    assert allow() is True

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        allow()


def test_exception_from_time_function_is_propagated(monkeypatch):
    def broken_time():
        raise RuntimeError("clock failed")

    monkeypatch.setattr(time, "time", broken_time)

    allow = rate_limiter(max_calls=1, period_seconds=10)

    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="clock failed"):
        allow()
```
